summary:
Profound hearing loss according to the World Health Organization is defined as having a hearing threshold greater than 90 decibels across all frequencies based on audiometric testing results and recognized medical literature supporting such classifications globally without significant controversy. The specific cutoff aligns consistently among authoritative health bodies including those referenced here which validate its use universally accepted standard within audiology practice worldwide. To summarize:

Profound hearing loss is classified when there are hearing thresholds exceeding 90 dB at multiple frequency levels per an international consensus established by the WHO through validated auditory assessments documented extensively in professional guidelines and research papers. This consistent benchmark ensures standardized diagnosis facilitating appropriate care protocols tailored specifically towards severe cases requiring intensive intervention strategies aimed improving quality life outcomes amongst affected individuals regardless regional variations impacting access healthcare services.
